title: ageratina bellidifolia
intro: Ageratina bellidifolia, also known as Mexican Mint or Hierba de la Reina, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Ageratina bellidifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine and as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
